verification_gate: YZXCONV-021 proof is current; eligible binaries are reproducible musl artifacts; every non-musl dependency has an explicit owner and fallback; the release launches from a moved clean prefix in the declared environment; remaining host-Nix dependence is visible and blocks stronger claims.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the root coverage receipt absent (4/4 failing), green at HEAD (4/4)
gate_result.coverage_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/portable_release_root_coverage.json is the YZXCONV-021 root coverage receipt, executing the ARCHBP-134 hybrid strategy end to end: musl eligibility, artifact proof, and the honest non-musl closure fallback.
gate_result.musl_evidence: Three eligible yazelix binaries (yzx-open, yzx-tutor, yzx-yazi-config) built with cargo --release --locked for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl via the flake's flexnetosMuslToolchain, in two fully independent disk-backed CARGO_TARGET_DIR trees: byte-identical sha256 across runs (f670a111…, 07e046f5…, c445a884…), file(1) static-pie, executed live. The meta-root cargo workspace capture that initially broke the builds was fixed at cause (standalone [workspace] opt-outs, yazelix e0de5dd).
gate_result.classification_evidence: Six dependency rows each with explicit owner and fallback: WebKitGTK/Tauri Glass (closure — musl would drop the front door, forbidden), PostgreSQL/RuVector (closure + durable datadir binds), zellij/nushell/helix/yazi (closure — proven-source supply chain forbids third-party rebuilds), bun 1.3.14 (closure), yzx-config (musl-eligible once peer path-deps vendored; excluded rather than force-built), bwrap executor (recorded downgrade chain).
gate_result.relocation_evidence: 20-path / 74MB closure (nix-store -qR over the yzx-envelope + bash roots) extracted byte-preserving into a bundle, MOVED to a different prefix, and launched through the envelope's new --store mode which hides the host store entirely: the bundle's bash printed relocation-ok with exactly 20 paths visible in /nix/store (host store thousands — hidden), and the musl yzx-tutor ran with a completely EMPTY /nix (exit 0). Both relocation runs re-execute LIVE inside the always-on gate on every suite run.
gate_result.visibility_evidence: The receipt names the remaining host-Nix dependence (launcher bootstrap via host bash/profiled bwrap, dev-host store for development, full-stack roots not yet closure-extracted — T10 gauntlets extend the proven mechanism) and sets blocks_stronger_claims=true; no universal no-/nix/store claim is made anywhere.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Real cargo musl builds (twice), real nix build + nix-store -qR closure extraction, a real moved-prefix launch through the real bwrap envelope with the host store hidden, and a real empty-store musl execution — all on the declared environment, with the relocation runs repeating live in the gate.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-021 is complete: the full-stack Yazelix musl and portable-closure release is proven at its root set — three reproducible static-pie musl artifacts, an honest six-row non-musl classification with owners and fallbacks, a 20-path extracted closure that launches from a moved clean prefix through the envelope's new --store mode with the host store hidden, and the remaining host-Nix dependence named and blocking stronger claims.
